State of California 
I 
Memora",dum 
To 
Memo to File 
Date 
March 5, 1982 
From 
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION 
Subject: 
Jeanne Pritchard 
N.A.C.H.O. - Intermediary for Californians for Obledo 
On January 28, 1982, I spoke with Juan Polito, an 
attorney for N.A.C.H.O., who asked for advice concerning 
the proper reporting of contributions received at a fund-
raiser sponsored by N.A.C.H.O.,ior Californians for Obledo 
and inadvertently deposited ~~N.A.C.H.O. in its checking 
account. 
N.A.C.H.O kept records on contributtors, and obtained 
required information on contributors of $100 or more. 
I advised that N.A.C.H.O. should write a check to 
Californians for Obledo and send an accompanying letter 
indicating that N.A.C.H.O. is acting as an intermediary for 
the contributions raised at a fundraiser for Obledo, and 
provide required information for contributions of $100 ~~ 
! ' 
Attached is a copy of a letter from David B. Castro, 
Co-Chairman of N.A.C.H.O. 
to Californians for Obledo, 
providing the required information 
;tTL 
~ ~"--r~,--,... , 
cc: 
Secretary of State's Office 


lalifornians For Ob1ed 
c/o Charlene Ortiz 
3746 Mount Dib10 Blvd. 
Suite #200 
La fayette, CA 
Re: Your Funds Which Were Inadvertently Deposited In N.A~e·:H.tl. A"c(6unt' ,. 
Dear Sirs: 
Please be informed that funds belonging to "Californians For Obledo" vlere inadver-
tently deposited in our N.A.C.H.O. account #08613-2553 at the Hesperian-La Playa 
branch of the Bank of America in Hayward. The first inadvertent deposit was on 
1-20-82 for the amount of $550.00. The second such deposit was on 1-22-82 for $585.00. 
Later that same date a third and final deposit for $650.00 was made. Attached is a 
copy of our bank deposit record verifying said deposits. Please note that the deposits 
made on or after 1-20-82 total $1,785.00. 
We discovered our error late on Friday, 1-22-82. But when we contacted the bank 
on Monday, 1-25-82 we were unable to retrieve your funds (checks) because they had 
already been processed. We have also been unable to secure copies of the deposited 
checks. 
However, we did keep accurate records by way of completed ticket stubs that you now 
have in your possession. Therefore, records are readily available to verify the 
source of each and every donation. If you will please review those stubs you should 
find that they total $1,785.00. Enclosed is a check in that amount. 
We have also checked with Miss Jeanne Pritchard, who is a consultant with The Fair 
Political Practices Commission in Sacramento (916-322-5660) and have been informed 
that we should also provide the fo11m'lin9 infol'mation on persons VJho donated S100.00 
or more: 
NAtv1E 
ADDRESS 
AND TELEPHONE NUMBER 
Art Moreno 
Felix Galaviz 
152 Laurel Ave. ,Hayward, CA 
786-6924 Classic Coffee(owner} $200.00 
32259 Valiant Way, Union City, CA 487-1329 Chabot Co11ege(A. Dean)$100.0C 
Charley Santana 
271 Goodrich St., Hayward, CA 
874-6781 Alameda Co. (Supervisor)S2 
Finally, we wish to emphsize that only funds deposited on or after 1-20-82 were 
erroneously deposited. Prior to that date, funds totaling $725.00 were proper1y 
deposited in our account. These funds represent the membership dues 
id by our 
participating organizations for membership in N.A.C.H.O. Enclosed are copies of 
receipt letters to those organizations thankinC] trleill ~or their 
rticioation.-
you have any questions regarding this matter please concan ille. 
Sincerely, 
David B. Castro 
Co-Chairman, N.A.C.H.O.

sections.question_synthetic: How should N.A.C.H.O. properly report and transfer contributions that were inadvertently deposited into N.A.C.H.O.'s checking account instead of being deposited directly to the Californians for Obledo campaign account?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: N.A.C.H.O. should write a check to Californians for Obledo with an accompanying letter indicating N.A.C.H.O. is acting as an intermediary for the fundraiser contributions, and provide required contributor information for all donations of $100 or more.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document is an internal FPPC memo with attached correspondence. No formal QUESTION/CONCLUSION sections present; advice is embedded in narrative memo form. The attached letter from David B. Castro demonstrates implementation of FPPC guidance. Some text quality issues from document scanning (e.g., 'Memora",dum', 'Supervisor)S2' appears truncated) but core information is extractable.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: How should N.A.C.H.O. properly report and transfer contributions that were inadvertently deposited into N.A.C.H.O.'s checking account instead of being deposited directly to the Californians for Obledo campaign account?

CONCLUSION: N.A.C.H.O. should write a check to Californians for Obledo with an accompanying letter indicating N.A.C.H.O. is acting as an intermediary for the fundraiser contributions, and provide required contributor information for all donations of $100 or more.

embedding.summary: FPPC memorandum documenting advice given to N.A.C.H.O. regarding the proper reporting and transfer of campaign contributions that were inadvertently deposited into N.A.C.H.O.'s account instead of Californians for Obledo's account, with N.A.C.H.O.'s follow-up letter providing the required donor information and check transfer.
